question: Will South Carolina enact a refundable EITC in 2026?
textDescription:
Resolves YES if any bill making South Carolina's state Earned Income Tax Credit at least partially refundable is signed into law by December 31, 2026.

For context, one such bill is H.3492, which would make 25% of the excess state EITC refundable. It is currently in the House Ways and Means Committee.

South Carolina's 2026 legislative session is scheduled to adjourn in mid-May.

Resolution source: SC Legislature bill tracker and governor's office.
newsShortReason: H.3492 has not progressed beyond committee, and the legislative session has ended.
newsKeyEvidence: H.3492 introduced on January 14, 2025, referred to House Ways and Means Committee, no further action reported. | South Carolina's 2026 legislative session adjourned in mid-May 2026. | Governor McMaster signed H.4216 into law on April 15, 2026, which restructured the state's income tax system. | No recent news indicates movement on H.3492 or similar bills. | No evidence of H.3492 being scheduled for a vote or committee hearing. | No statements from legislators or advocacy groups supporting H.3492's passage in 2026.
